description: Sessions — Cross-File Redaction Workspace. A session layer that groups files and LLM responses together, enabling cross-file entity referencing via a shared token namespace, back-and-forth masked-input/decoded-output workflows, and future MCP access scoping.

userStories.title: Sessions database schema and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sessions, session_items, and session_entries tables in SQLite so that sessions persist across app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: New `sessions` table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' (values: 'active' | 'archived'), created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')), updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))

New `session_items` join table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, session_id INTEGER NOT NULL (FK → sessions ON DELETE CASCADE), item_id INTEGER NOT NULL (FK → items ON DELETE CASCADE), added_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')), UNIQUE(session_id, item_id)

New `session_entries` table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, session_id INTEGER NOT NULL (FK → sessions ON DELETE CASCADE), entry_type TEXT NOT NULL ('input' | 'output'), source_item_id INTEGER (nullable FK → items ON DELETE SET NULL), raw_content TEXT NOT NULL, decoded_content TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
Add nullable session_id column to items table via idempotent ALTER TABLE migration (use existing error-catch pattern from db.rs)
CREATE INDEX on session_items(session_id) and session_items(item_id)
CREATE INDEX on session_entries(session_id)
All CREATE TABLE use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
Foreign keys cascade on delete for sessions → session_items and sessions → session_entries
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: Follow the existing idempotent migration pattern in db.rs — ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN wrapped in match with 'duplicate column name' error catch. New tables go in the execute_batch block. PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON is already set.

userStories.title: Tauri commands for session CRUD (create, list, get)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IPC commands to create, list, and get sessions from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: create_session(name: String) command that inserts into sessions table and returns the new session id as i64

list_sessions() command that returns Vec<SessionSummary> with fields: id, name, status, item_count (from COUNT on session_items), entry_count (from COUNT on session_entries), created_at, updated_at — ordered by updated_at DESC

get_session(session_id: i64) command that returns SessionDetail with: session fields + Vec<SessionEntryOutput> (id, entry_type, source_item_id, raw_content decrypted, decoded_content decrypted if present, created_at) + Vec<SessionItemOutput> (item id, name, file_type, entity_count, added_at)
SessionSummary, SessionDetail, SessionEntryOutput, SessionItemOutput are Serialize structs defined in commands.rs
Encrypted fields (raw_content, decoded_content) are decrypted using crypto::decrypt before returning — with fallback to raw string if decryption fails (same pattern as get_item)
All three commands registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: Use State<'_, DbState> for DB access. Lock mutex with db.0.lock(). Return Result<T, String> and map errors with .map_err(|e| e.to_string()). The list_sessions query should use LEFT JOIN + GROUP BY to get counts. Look at list_items and get_item in commands.rs for the existing pattern.

userStories.title: Tauri commands for session update, archive, and delete
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IPC commands to rename, archive, and delete sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: update_session(session_id: i64, name: String) command that updates the session name and updated_at timestamp
archive_session(session_id: i64) command that sets status to 'archived' and updates updated_at
unarchive_session(session_id: i64) command that sets status to 'active' and updates updated_at
delete_session(session_id: i64) command that deletes the session — cascade deletes session_items and session_entries rows, but items table rows remain untouched
All four commands registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: Simple UPDATE/DELETE statements. The cascade delete is handled by the ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key constraints from US-001. Still good to verify items are NOT deleted when a session is removed.

userStories.title: Tauri commands for session item management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need commands to add and remove items from sessions and query a session's entities.
acceptanceCriteria: add_item_to_session(session_id: i64, item_id: i64) command that inserts into session_items and updates sessions.updated_at — returns Ok(()) or error if already linked (UNIQUE constraint)
remove_item_from_session(session_id: i64, item_id: i64) command that deletes from session_items and updates sessions.updated_at

get_session_entities(session_id: i64) command that queries all entities across all items joined to the session via session_items, returns Vec<SessionEntityOutput> with fields: entity_type, original_value (decrypted), token — deduplicated by (entity_type, original_value), keeping the first token encountered
SessionEntityOutput is a Serialize struct in commands.rs
All three commands registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: For get_session_entities, join session_items → entities, decrypt original_value for each row, then deduplicate in Rust using a HashMap<(entity_type, decrypted_original_value), token>. The UNIQUE constraint on session_items(session_id, item_id) prevents duplicate links.

userStories.title: Tauri commands for session entries and decode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need commands to add output entries to a session and decode tokens in them.
acceptanceCriteria: add_session_entry(session_id: i64, entry_type: String, raw_content: String, source_item_id: Option<i64>) command that encrypts raw_content using crypto::encrypt, inserts into session_entries, updates sessions.updated_at, returns the new entry id

decode_session_entry(session_id: i64, entry_id: i64) command that: 1) loads all entities across all session items (via session_items join), 2) builds a token → decrypted_original_value map, 3) reads the entry's raw_content and decrypts it, 4) replaces all [[TYPE:HASH]] and [[TYPE_N]] tokens with original values using regex, 5) encrypts the decoded result and stores in decoded_content column, 6) returns the decoded text
Decoding handles both hash-style tokens ([[EMAIL:a3f2c1b9]]) and counter-style tokens ([[EMAIL_1]]) by matching against the entity map
Tokens with no match in the session vault are left as-is in the output (partial decode is valid)
Both commands registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: For the decode regex, use something like r'\[\[(\w+)[_:](\w+)\]\]' to match both formats. Build the token map from entities table (token column) to original_value (decrypted). Use regex replace_all with a closure that looks up each match in the map.

userStories.title: Token namespace reconciliation command
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a command that reconciles a file's entity tokens with a session's shared namespace when adding the file to a session.
acceptanceCriteria: reconcile_item_tokens(session_id: i64, item_id: i64) command that: 1) loads all entities from all OTHER items in the session, 2) builds a (entity_type, decrypted_original_value) → existing_token map, 3) loads the new item's entities, 4) for each entity in the new item: if (type, value) exists in map, rewrite its token to match; if not, assign a new token that doesn't conflict with existing session tokens, 5) rebuilds masked_content by replacing old tokens with new tokens, 6) updates item's masked_content and entities in a transaction
Reconciliation is idempotent — running it twice on the same item produces the same result
New tokens that don't conflict use the existing [[TYPE:HASH]] format from the masker
The command updates both the entities table rows and the item's masked_content column
All changes are wrapped in a single SQL transaction
Command registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: This is the most complex backend command. The token rewriting in masked_content is a string find-and-replace of old_token → new_token. Be careful about replacement order — replace longer tokens first to avoid partial matches. Use a transaction so the entities and masked_content update atomically.

userStories.title: Sessions sidebar navigation tab
userStories.description: As a user, I want a SESSIONS tab in the sidebar so I can switch between the file browser and session views.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar.svelte gains a third nav item 'SESSIONS' between BROWSE and SETTINGS (or after BROWSE)
ActiveView type updated to 'browse' | 'sessions' | 'settings' in Sidebar.svelte
Clicking SESSIONS sets activeView to 'sessions'
SESSIONS nav item uses a PixelIcon consistent with existing sidebar style (use 'chat' or add a new icon if needed — layers, conversation, or similar pixel art icon)
Active state highlighting works for the new tab (same --accent-orange pattern as BROWSE and SETTINGS)
+page.svelte: activeView state accepts 'sessions' value, when activeView is 'sessions' the center area shows a placeholder div with text 'Sessions' (to be replaced in next stories)
Existing BROWSE and SETTINGS views continue to work unchanged
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Update the ActiveView type export in Sidebar.svelte. If 'chat' icon doesn't exist in PixelIcon.svelte, add a new pixel art icon (24x24 SVG, fill=currentColor, shape-rendering=crispEdges). Look at the existing Sidebar.svelte pattern for nav items.

userStories.title: SessionList component with create, archive, and delete
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my sessions in a list so I can open, create, archive, or delete them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/SessionList.svelte that calls invoke('list_sessions') on mount and displays session cards
Each session card shows: name, status badge (active/archived), item count, entry count, last updated time (formatted)
'New Session' button at the top creates a session via invoke('create_session') with default name 'Session — [formatted date]' and refreshes the list
Sessions sorted by last updated (newest first), active before archived
Archive/unarchive toggle button per session (calls invoke('archive_session') or invoke('unarchive_session'))
Delete button per session with ConfirmDialog confirmation (reuse existing component), calls invoke('delete_session')
Archived sessions appear dimmed/muted
Empty state shown when no sessions exist with prompt to create one
Clicking a session card emits onselectsession callback with session id
SessionList rendered in +page.svelte center area when activeView is 'sessions' and no session is selected
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow the FileCard.svelte / FileBrowser.svelte patterns for card styling — dark background, border, rounded corners (0px for pixel art), status badge. Use the Svelte 5 callback props pattern (onselectsession, not createEventDispatcher). Import ConfirmDialog from $lib/components/ConfirmDialog.svelte.

userStories.title: Session detail feed view — basic structure and header
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a session and see its detail view with an editable name, back button, and chronological feed area.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/SessionDetail.svelte that accepts a sessionId prop and loads session data via invoke('get_session', { sessionId })

Header shows: back button (PixelIcon chevron-left or arrow) that emits onback callback, editable session name (inline text input that calls invoke('update_session') on blur/enter), session status badge
Main area is a scrollable vertical feed container that will hold entry cards (empty for now with placeholder text 'No entries yet. Add files or paste LLM output to get started.')
Feed area scrolls independently with overflow-y: auto
SessionDetail rendered in +page.svelte center area when activeView is 'sessions' and a selectedSessionId is set
+page.svelte gains selectedSessionId state (number | null), set by SessionList's onselectsession callback, cleared by SessionDetail's onback callback
All styling uses CSS custom properties from tokens.css
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This sets up the session detail shell. The feed entries, paste input, and drop zone are added in subsequent stories. Keep the component focused on the layout structure: header with back/name/status, scrollable feed body, and a bottom area (placeholder for the paste input). Load session data with invoke('get_session') which returns SessionDetail from US-002.

userStories.title: Session feed entry cards (input and output)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see input and output entries in the session feed with visual distinction between them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/SessionEntryCard.svelte that accepts an entry object (id, entry_type, raw_content, decoded_content, source_item_id, created_at) and optional item name
Input entries display: file/document PixelIcon, file name or 'Pasted text' label, timestamp, truncated preview of raw_content (first ~150 chars), styled with left accent border in --accent-orange

Output entries display: decode/chat PixelIcon, 'LLM Response' label, timestamp, truncated preview of decoded_content (or raw_content if not decoded), styled with left accent border in a complementary color (muted blue or green)
Cards are clickable — emit onselect callback with entry id
Output entries that have no decoded_content show a small 'Not decoded' indicator
SessionDetail.svelte renders SessionEntryCard for each entry from the loaded session data, ordered by created_at ascending (oldest first, newest at bottom)
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The session data from get_session already includes entries with decrypted content. Just render them as cards in the feed. Input entries use existing accent orange, output entries use a different color for contrast. Keep the card compact — this is a feed, not a file viewer.

userStories.title: Paste LLM output into session
userStories.description: As a user, I want a text input at the bottom of the session feed to paste LLM responses for decoding.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionDetail.svelte gains a fixed-to-bottom input area with a multi-line textarea and a submit button
Textarea placeholder: 'Paste LLM response here to decode...'
Submitting calls invoke('add_session_entry', { sessionId, entryType: 'output', rawContent: text, sourceItemId: null }), then calls invoke('decode_session_entry', { sessionId, entryId: newId })
After decode, the session data is refreshed and the new decoded entry appears in the feed
Textarea is cleared after successful submission
Submit button is disabled when textarea is empty
Input area has a frosted glass background (backdrop-filter blur + semi-transparent, same pattern as SearchBar)
Feed area has bottom padding to avoid entries being hidden behind the input area
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow the SearchBar pattern for the fixed-to-bottom positioning (position: absolute, bottom: 0, parent has position: relative). The submit flow is: add entry → decode entry → refresh session. Use async/await for the sequential invoke calls.

userStories.title: Add files to session — vault picker and drop zone
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add files to a session by selecting from my vault or dragging new files in.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionDetail.svelte has an 'Add Files' button in the header area that opens a vault picker overlay
Vault picker overlay shows existing vault items not yet in this session (filter out items already linked via session_items), as a simple list with checkboxes or clickable cards

Selecting an item calls invoke('add_item_to_session', { sessionId, itemId }), then invoke('reconcile_item_tokens', { sessionId, itemId }), creates an input entry via invoke('add_session_entry', { sessionId, entryType: 'input', rawContent: maskedContent, sourceItemId: itemId }), and refreshes session
The entire session detail view acts as a drop zone — dragging files shows a translucent overlay with 'Drop files to add to session' text
Dropped files go through the existing upload pipeline (reuse handleFileDrop logic from +page.svelte), then are linked to the current session with reconciliation
Supported file types: .txt, .md, .csv, .json, .pdf
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: For the vault picker, call invoke('list_items') to get all items, then filter out IDs already in the session. The drop zone overlay pattern is the same as FileBrowser's dragOver state. The upload pipeline is in +page.svelte — either extract the file ingestion functions to a shared module or call them from the parent and pass the resulting item ID down.

userStories.title: Entry detail popup with masked/decoded toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a feed entry to see its full content in a popup with toggle between masked and decoded views.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/SessionEntryPopup.svelte that accepts an entry object and session entities
For input entries: shows full file content with masked/original toggle (reuse highlighting patterns from FileViewer.svelte)
For output entries: shows raw content (with tokens highlighted) and decoded content (with original values) via a toggle
Output entries that haven't been decoded show a 'Decode' button that calls invoke('decode_session_entry') and refreshes
Popup renders as an overlay constrained to the center area (position: absolute, same pattern as FileViewer.svelte)
Popup has a header with entry type label, timestamp, and close button (PixelIcon close)
Dismissible via backdrop click, Escape key, or close button
Copy button to copy the displayed content to clipboard
SessionDetail.svelte: clicking a SessionEntryCard opens SessionEntryPopup with the selected entry
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse the overlay pattern from FileViewer.svelte (position: absolute, inset: 0, semi-transparent backdrop). For input entries, load the full item detail via invoke('get_item') using source_item_id. For output entries, the raw_content and decoded_content are already in the session data.
